sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, has been experiencing back pain for a while, with the pain worsening over the past month.
He reports that the pain is located in the lower back area and radiates down into the left leg, specifically to the big toe.
The pain is described as an electrical toothache and is rated 6 out of 10 at the moment, but can be as high as 13 out of 10 when it worsens.
He reports that coughing, sneezing, and standing exacerbate the pain, while laying down provides some relief.
He also reports numbness and tingling in both legs and weakness in his back and legs.
Past medical history: The patient had a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a fracture or other back injury.
He has been bedridden for a while due to the pain and has been using a cane to walk.
He has tried NSAIDs in the past without relief and has done physical therapy.
Review of systems: The patient reports that coughing and sneezing make the pain worse.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had a normal back exam with no bony abnormalities, redness, or bruising.
He had pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae and pain with flexion and extension of his back.
He had 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes were brisk and motor and sensory were intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; Lower back pain; Leg pain.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to be administered today, which will place anti-inflammatory medication at the site of inflammation.
I will refer the patient back to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in about a month to follow up on the treatment plan.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month to follow up on the treatment plan.
